rationale: Both titles convey the same full content: variant PV anatomy + enlarged cross-sectional area as predictors of long-term AF recurrence after cryoballoon PVI in paroxysmal AF, with a single-center cohort design. Title X uses title case and condenses the study descriptor to 'MRI-Based Cohort of 353 Patients', which is more informative than Y's generic 'single-center cohort study' — it specifies the imaging modality and sample size, giving the reader immediate quantitative context. Title Y spells out all abbreviations ('atrial fibrillation' twice in full), which slightly improves clarity for general readers but makes the title notably longer without adding information. For JICE, which favors concise, data-rich titles, X's approach of specifying MRI methodology and n=353 is more appealing and impactful. Y's repeated full spellings of 'atrial fibrillation' hurt economy without improving precision.
loser_weakness: Title Y omits the imaging modality (MRI) and sample size, replacing them with the uninformative label 'single-center cohort study'. The repeated full spelling of 'atrial fibrillation' inflates length without adding value, reducing overall appeal and economy for a JICE readership familiar with standard EP abbreviations.
